relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The high priest is taken from humans for humans before the Divine.
The priest knows the need for mercy himself.
Honor is received by Divine calling, not seized.
Messiah does not self-glorify into priesthood; Sonship is spoken over him.
Melchizedek’s order opens an age-long priesthood.
In flesh he prays with cries and tears to the One able to save from death.
Completed, he becomes source of age-long healing.
The Divine names him high priest in Melchizedek’s order.
The word is deep, but hearing has grown sluggish.
Beginnings must be learned again before teaching can mature.
Milk belongs to the untrained in justice’s word.
Solid food belongs to discernment trained by practice.

contemplative_note: Hebrews 5 is priesthood through shared weakness: not seized status, but tears, obedience, completion, and mature discernment trained by practice.
